      "lean_file_body": "",
      "notes": [
        "The required inequality (n choose k)^2 ‚Č• (n choose k-1)*(n choose k+1) is a known log-concavity property of binomial coefficients, but no checked Mathlib lemma with the exact signature was found. The fallback candidates do not provide this inequality directly. A proof would require combinatorial identities or induction not available from the visible context."
      ],
      "status": "cannot_prove_from_visible_context",
      "unit_key": "unit-001",
      "used_context": [
        "source: cor.lgv.binom-unimod",
        "hydrated_mathlib_context: Nat.choose_sq_ge_choose_mul_choose_succ not found"
      ]
    },
    {
      "declaration_names": [
        "vandermonde_identity"
      ],
      "lean_file_body": "open Finset

theorem vandermonde_identity (a b n : ‚Ąē) :
    Nat.choose (a + b) n = ‚ąĎ k in range (n + 1), Nat.choose a k * Nat.choose b (n - k) :=
by
  refine (Nat.add_comm a b).symm ‚Ėł ?_
  -- Use the standard Vandermonde identity from Mathlib

  -- We can use the combinatorial argument: (1+x)^a * (1+x)^b = (1+x)^(a+b)
  -- This is a standard result; we can use the existing lemma `Nat.choose_add`?
